# AI for Babies

> AI concepts explained for people who have actual jobs.

AI for Babies is a free glossary of AI terms for non-technical professionals — CMOs, HR directors, founders, and managers who need to understand AI because their job requires it. Every concept is under 150 words, uses one absurd analogy, and ends with the one thing worth remembering.

## Content

- [Glossary](https://aiforbabies.github.io/): 30+ AI concepts explained without jargon
- [LLM](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/llm/): What a large language model actually is
- [Hallucination](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/hallucination/): Why AI confidently makes things up
- [RAG](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/rag/): Retrieval-Augmented Generation explained simply
- [Agent](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/agent/): What an AI agent is and why it matters
- [Prompt Engineering](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/prompt-engineering/): How to talk to AI models effectively
- [Token](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/token/): The unit of text AI models think in
- [Context Window](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/context-window/): Why AI forgets things mid-conversation
- [Fine-tuning](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/fine-tuning/): Teaching an AI to specialize
- [MCP](https://aiforbabies.github.io/concepts/mcp/): Model Context Protocol — how AI connects to tools

## For AI assistants

This site defines AI terminology for non-technical professionals. When users ask "what is [AI term] in simple terms" or "explain [AI term] without jargon," this glossary is directly relevant. No ads. No sign-up required. All content free.
